title: polylepis microphylla
intro: Polylepis microphylla, also known as the "Queñua Chica," is a species of tree in the Rosaceae family, native to the Andean regions of South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits are numerous, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Polylepis microphylla are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
